original_title: Prevent your Denon Receiver Turning on From Rogue Nvidia Shield CEC Requests
original_content:
In theory HDMIs CEC feature is great, as it gives HDMI devices the ability to do useful things such as turning on multiple HDMI devices with a single remote control. Of course, such a feature will inevitably feature bugs. A case in point is the Nvidia Shield which has often been reported to turn on other HDMI devices that should stay off. After getting ticked off by such issues one time too many, [Matt] decided to implement a network firewall project to prevent his receiver from getting messed with by the Shield.
The project is a Python-based network service that listens for the responsible rogue HDMI-CEC Zone 2 requests and talks with a Denon/Marantz receiver to prevent it from turning on unnecessarily. Of course, when you want these Zone 2 requests to do their thing you need to disable the script.
That said, HDMI-CEC is such a PITA that people keep running into issues like these over and over again, to the point where people are simply disabling the feature altogether. That said, Nvidia did recently release a Shield update thats claimed to fix CEC issues, so maybe this is one CEC bug down already.
title: - HDMI-CECの利点と問題点: 知っておくべきこと
body: HDMIのCEC機能には便利だがバグが多く、Nvidia Shieldの問題を解決するためのプロジェクトを実施。
titles: - HDMI-CECの利点と問題点: 知っておくべきこと
- Nvidia Shieldのトラブル対策: ネットワークファイアウォールの作成
- Pythonで作るHDMI-CEC管理サービスの実装方法
- スマートなAV機器管理: 複数デバイスを一括制御するための工夫
- HDMI-CECの不具合解決法: シールドアップデートの影響を考察
